name: Expeditions: Viking
description: Circa 790 AD. A small band of Norse warriors lands on the shores of England. History may have forgotten their names, but their actions live on. As the chieftain of your clan, let ambition guide your choices in the party-based RPG Expeditions: Viking. How will history remember you?
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS *: Windows 7
  • Processor: Intel Core2 Quad Q9400 2.66 GHz
  • Memory: 4 GB RAM
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GeForce GTS 450
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Storage: 12 GB available space
  • Sound Card: DirectX Compatible Sound Card
comment1: This is a pretty good game for fans of RPG's and turn based strategy. There are a few flaws with the combat system that make the early going very difficult (enemy archers shoot through cover with ease, many abilities don't work as advertised). It does a good job capturing Viking flavor. I give it a B-, so I recommend buying it on sale.
comment2:
Really nice tactical game, if a bit short unless you decide to clear all points of interest on a map

Also time limits aren't fun
comment3:
The combat is a chaotic mess and seems bugged at times,
I will say if you exclude the horrible try at a different way of doing tactical combat,
it's a very decent game that will appeal to players that like to delve into an RP game
heavily influenced and involving the viking theme.

The issue I have with the combat is their arbitrary circles of colors that they use to
define what you can and can't do, along with ...if the game bugs out and you can't do
an action then you can't undo it, you just waste your action.
I am not saying every turn based combat game should use the color and icon scheme
in Baldurs Gate 3, but as a reference to a clean understandable UI for turn based combat
it stands out as a good reference for other devs.
